sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Gregory, presents with a nonhealing foot ulcer on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, which has been present for approximately two months.
The ulcer is currently red, draining, and swollen, with some edema present.
The patient reports throbbing pain, but no distinct pain, and states that the ulcer started to drain and become red recently.
The patient has a history of walking barefoot on the beach, specifically at Dewey Beach, and reports not having taken his prescribed antibiotics yet.
His diabetes is not well-controlled, with an A1c of 10, and he has not checked his blood sugar regularly.
Past medical history:
The patient has diabetes, and his A1c is 10.
Review of systems:
The patient reports throbbing pain in the foot, but no distinct pain.
Current medications:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's vital signs are normal.
Physical exam: The patient has a one by two inch circular wound on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, proximal to the fifth MTP joint, with redness, drainage, edema, and fluid present.
The patient has some increased pressure when touching the wound, but no distinct pain.
Test Results:
The patient had an x-ray of the right foot, which showed no evidence of osteomyelitis.
Assessment / Problem List:
The patient has a diabetic foot ulcer, which is likely infected, with an assessment of confirmed.
Problem list: Diabetic foot ulcer; infection; uncontrolled diabetes; poor wound healing.
Plan:
I prescribed clindamycin 400 milligrams, to be taken twice a day for seven days, to treat the infection.
I will also perform a debridement to remove dead tissue from the wound.
I will order an ABI to determine the blood supply to the foot.
I will provide a surgical shoe for the patient to wear to reduce pressure on the foot.
I will see the patient back in two weeks to assess the wound and potentially perform another debridement.
Follow-up:
I will see the patient in two weeks to assess the wound and determine the need for further treatment.
